channel.title: AGILE SAPIENS – Legals
channel.description: Recent content in Legals on AGILE SAPIENS

item.title: AI Transparency Statement
item.author: info@folkup.app (FolkUp Ecosystem)
item.description:
AI Transparency Statement

Required by: EU AI Act Article 50 Effective Date: March 26, 2026 Publisher: FolkUp Ecosystem

Automated Tool Usage Disclosure

In compliance with Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (EU AI Act), Article 50, we transparently disclose that automated tools have been used in the development of content for the AGILE SAPIENS monograph.

Scope of Automated Tool Usage

Automated tools are utilized for the following purposes:

✅ Research and Analysis Support

  • Information gathering and preliminary research
  • Source identification and fact-checking assistance
  • Data analysis and pattern recognition

✅ Content Development Assistance

  • Editorial drafting support
  • Style and voice consistency maintenance
  • Content structuring and organization

✅ Quality Assurance Support

  • Grammar and language optimization
  • Fact verification assistance
  • Citation and reference formatting

Human Oversight Guarantee

🛡️ Mandatory Human Review

Every piece of published content undergoes comprehensive human editorial review by qualified professionals before publication.

🛡️ No Automated Publication

Zero automated publication: No content is published without human oversight, fact-checking, and explicit approval.

🛡️ Editorial Accountability

All published content includes:

  • reviewed_by: Name of human editor responsible
  • review_date: Date of editorial review completion
  • sources: Verified source references

Quality Standards

Banking-Level Accuracy

  • All factual claims verified through multiple independent sources
  • Scientific rigor maintained throughout all content
  • Professional editorial standards applied consistently

Voice and Authenticity

  • “Harari meets Dilbert” voice archetype maintained by human editors
  • Authentic scientific popular writing style preserved
  • Original analysis and insights developed through human expertise

Transparency Commitment

We commit to:

  • Clear labeling of automated tool usage where applicable
  • Continuous disclosure of our editorial processes
  • Regular updates to this transparency statement as processes evolve
  • Responsive communication regarding our methodology

Contact for Transparency Inquiries

For questions about our use of automated tools or editorial processes:

  • Editorial Transparency: editor@folkup.app
  • General Inquiries: legal@folkup.app
  • Project Information: info@folkup.app

Regulatory Compliance

This disclosure fulfills requirements under:

  • EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689), Article 50
  • GDPR transparency obligations
  • Portuguese consumer protection standards

Note: This statement applies to the AGILE SAPIENS monograph and associated digital content. Other FolkUp Ecosystem publications may have separate transparency disclosures.

Last Updated: March 26, 2026 Document Version: 1.0

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.author: info@folkup.app (FolkUp Ecosystem)
item.description:
Privacy Policy

Last updated: March 26, 2026 Effective date: March 26, 2026

1. Introduction

AGILE SAPIENS (“we,” “our,” or “us”) is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard information when you access our scientific monograph website at sapiens.folkup.life (the “Site”).

Controller Information:

  • Data Controller: FolkUp Ecosystem
  • Contact Email: privacy@folkup.app
  • Jurisdiction: European Union, Portugal
  • Supervisory Authority: Comissão Nacional de Proteção de Dados (CNPD) - Portugal

2. Legal Basis

This privacy policy is implemented in accordance with:

  • Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation - GDPR)
  • Lei n.º 58/2019 (Portuguese GDPR implementation law)
  • Directive 2002/58/EC (ePrivacy Directive)

3. Information We Collect

3.1 Automatically Collected Information

When you visit our site, we may automatically collect:

  • IP address (anonymized)
  • Browser type and version
  • Operating system
  • Referring website
  • Pages visited and time spent
  • Date and time of access

Legal Basis: Legitimate interests (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)) for website functionality and security.

3.2 Information You Provide

  • Email address (if you contact us)
  • Name (if you provide it voluntarily)
  • Any content you submit through contact forms

Legal Basis: Consent (GDPR Art. 6(1)(a)) or legitimate interests for responding to inquiries.

4. How We Use Information

We use collected information to:

  • Provide and maintain the Site
  • Improve user experience
  • Respond to your inquiries
  • Ensure site security
  • Comply with legal obligations

Retention Period: Server logs are retained for 30 days maximum. Contact information is retained for 2 years or until you request deletion.

5. Data Sharing

We do not sell, trade, or rent your personal information. We may share information only:

  • With hosting providers (Hetzner Online GmbH, Germany - GDPR compliant)
  • When required by law or legal process
  • To protect our rights or safety

Data Transfers: Any data transfers outside the EU are protected by appropriate safeguards under GDPR Chapter V.

6. Your Rights

Under GDPR, you have the right to:

  • Access your personal data (Art. 15)
  • Rectification of inaccurate data (Art. 16)
  • Erasure (“right to be forgotten”) (Art. 17)
  • Restrict processing (Art. 18)
  • Data portability (Art. 20)
  • Object to processing (Art. 21)
  • Withdraw consent at any time (Art. 7)

To exercise these rights, contact: privacy@folkup.app

Response Time: We will respond within 30 days as required by GDPR Art. 12.

7. Cookies and Tracking

This site uses cookies and similar technologies as described in our Cookie Policy.

7.1 Essential Cookies

  • Cookie consent preferences (1 year retention)
  • Theme preferences (dark/light mode, 1 year retention)

Legal Basis: Legitimate interests (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)) for website functionality.

7.2 Optional Third-Party Services

  • Ko-fi donation widget: Only loads with your consent
  • Analytics: Not currently implemented; would require explicit consent if added

7.3 Your Cookie Rights

You can:

  • Manage cookie preferences via our consent banner
  • Withdraw consent at any time using the cookie settings button
  • Block cookies through your browser settings

See our Cookie Policy for detailed information.

8. Security

We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction, in accordance with GDPR Art. 32.

9. Data Protection Officer

For data protection inquiries, you may contact our Data Protection Officer at: dpo@folkup.app

10. Complaints

If you believe we have not complied with data protection laws, you may:

  1. Contact us directly at privacy@folkup.app
  2. Lodge a complaint with CNPD: www.cnpd.pt

11. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated “Last modified” date. For material changes, we will provide additional notice.

12. Contact Information

For privacy-related questions, contact:

  • Email: privacy@folkup.app
  • Data Controller: FolkUp Ecosystem
  • Supervisory Authority: CNPD (Comissão Nacional de Proteção de Dados)

This policy is governed by Portuguese law and EU regulations.

item.title: Cookie Policy
item.author: info@folkup.app (FolkUp Ecosystem)
item.description:
Cookie Policy

Last updated: March 26, 2026 Effective date: March 26, 2026

1. Introduction

This Cookie Policy explains how AGILE SAPIENS (“we,” “our,” or “us”) uses cookies and similar tracking technologies on our website at sapiens.folkup.life (the “Site”).

This policy should be read together with our Privacy Policy.

Controller Information:

  • Data Controller: FolkUp Ecosystem
  • Contact Email: privacy@folkup.app
  • Jurisdiction: European Union, Portugal

2. What Are Cookies

Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help websites remember your preferences and provide functionality.

3. Types of Cookies We Use

3.1 Essential Cookies

These cookies are necessary for the website to function properly:

Cookie Name Purpose Duration Legal Basis
cookie-consent Stores your cookie consent preferences 1 year Legitimate interest (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f))
theme-preference Remembers your dark/light mode choice 1 year Legitimate interest

3.2 Analytics Cookies (Opt-in)

These cookies help us understand how visitors use our site:

Cookie Name Provider Purpose Duration Legal Basis
Currently not implemented N/A N/A N/A N/A

Note: If we implement analytics in the future, you will be asked for explicit consent before these cookies are set.

3.3 Third-Party Cookies

Ko-fi Support Widget

When you interact with our Ko-fi support button:

  • Provider: Ko-fi.com
  • Purpose: Enable donation functionality
  • Consent: Required before loading
  • Privacy Policy: Ko-fi Privacy Policy

Social Media Embeds (if any)

Currently not implemented. If added in the future, explicit consent will be required.

4. Cookie Consent

4.1 Your Choices

  • Essential cookies: Cannot be disabled as they’re necessary for site functionality
  • Non-essential cookies: Require your explicit consent
  • Third-party widgets: Blocked until you give consent

4.2 Managing Consent

You can:

  • Change your cookie preferences using our consent banner
  • Block cookies through your browser settings
  • Withdraw consent at any time by clearing cookies or updating preferences

4.3 Browser Settings

Most browsers allow you to:

  • Block all cookies
  • Block third-party cookies only
  • Clear existing cookies
  • Receive notifications before cookies are set

For browser-specific instructions:

  • Chrome
  • Firefox
  • Safari
  • Edge

5. Impact of Blocking Cookies

Essential Cookies

Blocking these may result in:

  • Loss of theme preferences
  • Repeated cookie consent prompts
  • Reduced functionality

Non-Essential Cookies

Blocking these will not affect core site functionality.

6. Data Retention

  • Cookie consent: 1 year or until withdrawn
  • Theme preferences: 1 year or until cleared
  • Session data: Cleared when browser is closed

7. International Transfers

Ko-fi services may involve data transfers outside the EU. Ko-fi implements appropriate safeguards under GDPR Chapter V.

8. Your Rights

Under GDPR, you have rights regarding your personal data processed through cookies:

  • Access your data (Art. 15)
  • Rectification of inaccurate data (Art. 16)
  • Erasure (“right to be forgotten”) (Art. 17)
  • Restrict processing (Art. 18)
  • Data portability (Art. 20)
  • Object to processing (Art. 21)
  • Withdraw consent (Art. 7)

Contact us at privacy@folkup.app to exercise these rights.

9. Children’s Privacy

Our site is not directed to children under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children through cookies.

10. Updates to This Policy

We may update this Cookie Policy to reflect changes in our practices or legal requirements. Updates will be posted with a revised “Last updated” date.

For material changes affecting your rights, we will:

  • Update our consent banner
  • Notify users via the website
  • Request renewed consent where required

11. Contact Information

For cookie-related questions:

  • Email: privacy@folkup.app
  • Data Controller: FolkUp Ecosystem
  • Subject: Cookie Policy Inquiry

12. Legal Framework

This Cookie Policy complies with:

  • Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR)
  • Directive 2002/58/EC (ePrivacy Directive)
  • Lei n.º 58/2019 (Portuguese implementation)

Supervisory Authority: Comissão Nacional de Proteção de Dados (CNPD) - Portugal Website: www.cnpd.pt

item.title: Terms of Use
item.author: info@folkup.app (FolkUp Ecosystem)
item.description:
Terms of Use

Last updated: March 26, 2026 Effective date: March 26, 2026

1. Agreement to Terms

By accessing and using the AGILE SAPIENS website at sapiens.folkup.life (the “Site”), you accept and agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use this Site.

Site Owner: FolkUp Ecosystem Contact: legal@folkup.app

2. About AGILE SAPIENS

AGILE SAPIENS is a scientific popular monograph exploring the future of work in the age of artificial intelligence. The content is provided for educational, research, and informational purposes.

3. Use License

3.1 Content License

The monograph content is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0). You are free to:

  • Share and redistribute the material
  • Adapt, remix, transform, and build upon the material
  • Use for any purpose, including commercial

Attribution Required: You must provide appropriate credit, indicate changes made, and link to the license.

3.2 Website Code

The website implementation is licensed under MIT License. See the LICENSE file for full terms.

3.3 Restrictions

You may not:

  • Use content in a way that suggests author endorsement without permission
  • Remove or obscure attribution requirements
  • Violate applicable laws or regulations
  • Interfere with Site functionality or security

4. Content Disclaimer

4.1 Educational Purpose

This monograph is provided for educational and informational purposes. Views expressed are those of the authors and do not constitute professional advice.

4.2 Accuracy

While we strive for accuracy, we make no warranties about the completeness, reliability, or accuracy of information. Content is provided “as is.”

4.3 Scientific Popular Format

This is popular science content designed for general audiences. For academic research, please consult peer-reviewed sources and primary research.

5. AI Transparency (EU AI Act Article 50)

In compliance with EU AI Act Article 50, we disclose that:

  • This content has been developed with the assistance of automated tools
  • All content undergoes editorial review and fact-checking
  • Editorial workflow includes human oversight and verification
  • Content is marked with reviewed_by and review_date in metadata where applicable

See our Editorial Workflow documentation for detailed processes.

6. Intellectual Property

6.1 AGILE SAPIENS Trademark

“AGILE SAPIENS” is a trademark of FolkUp Ecosystem. Use of the name requires permission except for attribution under the content license.

6.2 Third-Party Content

The Site may reference third-party content. All third-party intellectual property rights are respected and acknowledged.

7. Privacy

Your privacy is important to us. Please review our Privacy Policy, which governs data collection and use.

8. Limitation of Liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law:

  • The Site is provided “as is” without warranties
  • We are not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages
  • Our total liability shall not exceed €100

Portuguese Law: These limitations comply with Portuguese Consumer Protection Law and EU regulations.

9. Indemnification

You agree to indemnify FolkUp Ecosystem against claims arising from your use of the Site or violation of these terms.

10. Termination

We may terminate or suspend access to the Site at any time for any reason, including violation of these terms.

11. Changes to Terms

We reserve the right to modify these terms. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated date. Continued use constitutes acceptance of modified terms.

12. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

12.1 Governing Law

These Terms are governed by Portuguese law and European Union regulations.

12.2 Dispute Resolution

  1. Initial Contact: Disputes should first be addressed to legal@folkup.app
  2. Jurisdiction: Portuguese courts have exclusive jurisdiction
  3. EU Online Dispute Resolution: EU consumers may use the ODR platform: ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr

12.3 Consumer Rights

If you are an EU consumer, these terms do not affect your statutory consumer rights under applicable law.

13. Severability

If any provision of these terms is invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions remain in effect.

14. Contact Information

For questions about these Terms of Use:

  • Email: legal@folkup.app
  • Address: FolkUp Ecosystem
  • Governing Law: Portuguese law, EU regulations

Language: These terms are written in English. In case of conflict with translations, the English version prevails.

item.title: Editorial Workflow
item.author: info@folkup.app (FolkUp Ecosystem)
item.description:
Editorial Workflow

Document Version: 1.0 Last Updated: March 26, 2026 Compliance: EU AI Act Article 50

1. Overview

This document describes the editorial process for AGILE SAPIENS monograph content, ensuring transparency, accuracy, and compliance with EU AI Act requirements.

2. Editorial Standards

2.1 Quality Framework

  • Scientific Accuracy: All factual claims verified through multiple sources
  • Banking-Level Standards: Zero tolerance for inaccuracies in published content
  • Voice Consistency: “Harari meets Dilbert” archetype maintained throughout
  • Controversy Calibration: Target 78% for optimal engagement while maintaining credibility

2.2 Content Categories

  • Verified: Fully fact-checked, multiple independent sources
  • Partially Verified: Key facts checked, some elements pending verification
  • Unverified: Preliminary content, requires full verification before publication
  • Draft: Work in progress, not ready for review

3. AI Transparency (EU AI Act Article 50)

3.1 AI Tool Disclosure

In compliance with EU AI Act Article 50, we disclose that automated tools are used in the content development process for:

  • Research assistance and fact-checking support
  • Editorial drafting and content structuring
  • Style consistency and voice calibration

3.2 Human Oversight

Critical Requirement: All content published undergoes mandatory human review and approval by qualified editors.

No Automated Publication: No content is published without human editorial oversight and fact-verification.

4. Editorial Process

4.1 Content Creation Phase

  1. Research: Primary and secondary source compilation
  2. Drafting: Initial content creation with automated tool assistance
  3. Self-Review: Author preliminary check
  4. Quality Gate 1: Fact verification and source validation

4.2 Review Phase

  1. Editorial Review: Professional editor evaluation

    • Factual accuracy verification
    • Style and voice consistency check
    • Scientific rigor assessment
    • Legal compliance verification

  2. Project Manager Review (Borges):

    • Strategic alignment with monograph objectives
    • Controversy calibration assessment
    • Target audience appropriateness

  3. Final Approval: Publication authorization

4.3 Publication Phase

  1. Metadata Completion: Required frontmatter fields
  2. Final Verification: Pre-publication checklist
  3. Publication: Content goes live
  4. Post-Publication Monitoring: Ongoing accuracy maintenance

5. Frontmatter Requirements

Every published article must include the following metadata:

---
title: "Chapter Title"
description: "Brief chapter description"
date: 2026-03-26
weight: 10
status: verified              # verified | partially_verified | unverified | draft
confidence: high             # high | medium | low
reviewed_by: "Editor Name"   # REQUIRED for status: verified/partially_verified
review_date: 2026-03-26     # REQUIRED - date of editorial review
sources:                    # REQUIRED - list of sources
  - "Source 1 URL/Citation"
  - "Source 2 URL/Citation"
tags: ["AI", "Future of Work", "Automation"]
category: "analysis"
---

6. Source Verification Standards

6.1 Source Quality Requirements

  • Primary Sources: Academic papers, government reports, corporate disclosures
  • Secondary Sources: Reputable news outlets, industry analyses
  • Minimum Sources: 2 independent sources for any factual claim
  • Source Dating: Sources must be current within 2 years unless historical context

6.2 Fact-Checking Protocol

  1. Initial Verification: All claims checked during drafting
  2. Cross-Reference: Multiple source verification for key facts
  3. Expert Consultation: Subject matter expert review for complex topics
  4. Update Monitoring: Ongoing monitoring of source validity

7. Quality Assurance

7.1 Review Requirements

  • Editorial Review: Mandatory for all published content
  • Review Documentation: reviewed_by and review_date fields required
  • Review Standards: Banking-level accuracy and completeness

7.2 Continuous Improvement

  • Feedback Integration: Reader feedback incorporated into future updates
  • Process Refinement: Regular workflow optimization based on outcomes
  • Training Updates: Editorial team kept current with best practices

8. Legal Compliance

8.1 EU AI Act Compliance

  • Transparency Requirements: Clear disclosure of AI tool usage
  • Human Oversight: Mandatory human review and approval
  • Quality Standards: Maintained to professional editorial standards

8.2 Content Licensing

  • Content License: CC BY 4.0 - freely shareable with attribution
  • Attribution Requirements: Proper credit and license acknowledgment required
  • Commercial Use: Permitted under CC BY 4.0 terms

9. Contact Information

For editorial process inquiries:

  • Editorial Contact: editor@folkup.app
  • Project Manager: borges@folkup.app
  • Legal/Compliance: legal@folkup.app

10. Document Updates

This workflow document is updated as needed to reflect process improvements and regulatory changes. All updates are dated and version-controlled.

Next Review Date: June 26, 2026

item.title: UGC Moderation Policy
item.author: info@folkup.app (FolkUp Ecosystem)
item.description:
UGC Moderation Policy

Effective Date: March 26, 2026 | Version: 1.0.0

This page summarizes our approach to moderating user-generated content. The full policy is maintained in our governance documentation.

Our Commitment

AGILE SAPIENS welcomes constructive discourse, including strong disagreement with our thesis. We moderate content to ensure safety and legal compliance, not to suppress opposing viewpoints.

What We Welcome

  • Evidence-based counter-arguments
  • Corrections with supporting sources
  • Real-world experience reports (anonymized where appropriate)
  • Constructive criticism of our methodology or conclusions
  • Alternative perspectives from industry practitioners

What We Remove

  • Illegal content (hate speech, incitement to violence)
  • Personal attacks and harassment
  • Doxxing or publishing private information
  • Spam and commercial solicitations
  • Defamatory statements presented as fact without evidence
  • Copyright-infringing material

How Moderation Works

  1. All submissions are reviewed before publication
  2. Content is classified by topic sensitivity and legal risk
  3. Decisions are documented with rationale
  4. Users are notified of rejection with explanation
  5. Appeals are handled by senior editorial staff

Your Rights (GDPR)

  • Access: Request a copy of your submitted content and moderation history
  • Rectification: Correct factual errors in your submissions
  • Erasure: Request deletion of your contributions
  • Objection: Challenge moderation decisions

To exercise these rights, contact: legal@folkup.app

Response Times

Content Type Target Response
Standard comments 48 hours
Controversial topics 24 hours
Legal concerns 4 hours (initial acknowledgment)

Transparency

  • Moderation decisions are logged and auditable
  • Automated filters are disclosed per EU AI Act requirements
  • Human review is available for all automated decisions

Contact

  • Moderation questions: moderation@folkup.app
  • Legal / GDPR requests: legal@folkup.app
  • General inquiries: info@folkup.app

For the complete moderation policy, see our governance documentation. This policy should be read alongside our Terms of Use, Privacy Policy, and Editorial Workflow.
